paper_title: Hydrothermal Sediment Sampling and Analysis at Spathi Bay

methods.description: Collection of sediment samples from Spathi Bay using SCUBA-divers to study hydrothermal activity.

steps.description: Sediment samples were collected by SCUBA-divers in 10 m water depth at Spathi Bay in May 2012.
Temperature was measured in situ in 5 cm intervals by a handheld temperature-probe in a custom-built underwater housing.
steps.searchable_summary: measure in situ temperature at 5 cm intervals

steps.description: Four sediment cores were retrieved along a transect with increasing temperatures.
steps.searchable_summary: retrieve sediment cores along temperature transect

steps.description: An additional core was taken a few meters away from the white patches, in an area presumably free of any major vent influence.
steps.searchable_summary: collect reference sediment core away from vent influence

steps.description: Sediment cores were sliced into 2 cm sections and kept at −20°C for lipid analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: slice sediment cores and store for lipid analysis

methods.description: Use of microsensors to measure oxygen, H2S, and pH in situ at various sediment stations.

steps.description: Clark-type oxygen, H2S, and pH microelectrodes were used for in situ measurements at all stations except for S2.
steps.searchable_summary: use microsensors for in situ measurements

steps.description: Sensors were mounted on an autonomous profiling lander deployed at the sediment-water interface.
steps.searchable_summary: deploy sensors on profiling lander at sediment-water interface

steps.description: Depth profiles were recorded with a spatial resolution of 100 µm, and sensors were allowed to equilibrate at each depth for 5 s before the signal was recorded.
Triplicate readings were averaged from each depth.

methods.description: Calibration procedures for oxygen, H2S, and pH microsensors prior to in situ measurements.

steps.description: Oxygen microsensors were calibrated by a two-point calibration using aerated and anoxic seawater.
steps.searchable_summary: calibrate oxygen microsensors with two-point method

steps.description: H2S measurements were calibrated with a 4-5 point calibration in anoxic seawater of pH lower than 2.
steps.searchable_summary: calibrate H2S microsensors with multi-point method

steps.description: pH sensors were 2-point-calibrated using commercial buffer solutions.
steps.searchable_summary: calibrate pH sensors with buffer solutions
